seoTitle: How to Add Expense Categories in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Learn how to add expense categories in QuickBooks Online so every purchase is sorted correctly and your profit and loss report stays useful.
intro: Well-defined expense categories make your profit and loss statement actually mean something, instead of lumping everything together. This guide covers how to add new expense categories in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open the chart of accounts
steps.body: Go to your chart of accounts where expense categories are created and managed.

steps.heading: Add a new account
steps.body: Select the option to create a new account and choose expense as the account type.

steps.heading: Choose a detail type
steps.body: Pick the detail type that best matches the kind of expense you're tracking, such as office supplies or utilities.

steps.heading: Name and save the category
steps.body: Give the category a clear, specific name and save it so it's available when categorizing transactions.

faq.question: How specific should my expense categories be?
faq.answer: Categories should be specific enough to be useful in reports, but not so granular that they become hard to manage.

faq.question: Can I create subcategories under an expense category?
faq.answer: Yes, you can nest expense categories under a parent account for more detailed breakdowns.

faq.question: What happens if I delete an expense category by mistake?
faq.answer: You can typically restore an inactive category, though transactions tied to it may need review afterward.

faq.question: Can I rename an expense category later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can rename a category at any time and it will update on past transactions using it.
